diagnosis.reason: Moderate lighting conditions and shadow presence may affect maturity estimation accuracy.
evidence_points: Maturity corrected from 0.68 to 0.81, indicating lighting-based adjustment
Scene average brightness is low (0.45) for morning imaging
Shadow ratio of 0.31 suggests partial occlusion or directional lighting
risks: Underestimation of ripeness due to shadows on fruit surface
Overcorrection by PINN in low-brightness conditions
Reduced generalization if lighting differs significantly from training data
recommendations: Supplement with diffuse lighting during morning captures
Validate corrected maturity against ground-truth samples under similar lighting
Monitor consistency of corrections across multiple detections in the same scene

decision.message: Consider adding diffuse lighting and manually verify ripeness before harvest.
decision.rationale: Low morning light and shadows may reduce maturity estimation accuracy.

diagnosis.reason: Moderate lighting conditions and shadow presence may affect maturity estimation accuracy despite high detection confidence.
evidence_points: Maturity corrected upward from 0.68 to 0.81, suggesting lighting compensation was applied
Scene average brightness is low (0.45) for morning imaging
Shadow ratio of 0.31 indicates partial occlusion or directional lighting
risks: Underestimation of true maturity due to shadowed regions
Overcorrection by PINN in low-light conditions
Morning dew or glare not captured in metadata but potentially affecting reflectance
recommendations: Validate maturity estimate with ground-truth sampling
Consider re-imaging under more diffuse lighting if possible
Monitor consistency of correction magnitude across similar lighting conditions

decision.message: Delay harvest; use supplemental lighting and verify maturity manually.
decision.rationale: Low ambient light and shadow may compromise maturity accuracy despite high detection confidence.

diagnosis.reason: Consistent lighting conditions and correction pattern across repeated detections, but low brightness and shadow presence limit reliability.
evidence_points: Maturity consistently corrected from 0.68 to 0.81 across multiple timestamps
Scene average brightness remains low (0.45) typical of early morning imaging
Shadow ratio of 0.31 persists, indicating stable but suboptimal lighting geometry
High detection confidence (0.93) suggests robust object identification despite lighting
risks: Persistent shadows may mask true color cues for ripeness
PINN correction may not fully compensate for directional morning light
Potential overreliance on correction model if lighting deviates from training distribution
recommendations: Deploy diffuse supplemental lighting during morning captures
Cross-validate corrected maturity with physical sampling
Track correction stability across more time points to assess model consistency

decision.message: Delay harvest; use supplemental diffuse lighting and manually verify ripeness.
decision.rationale: Low morning brightness and persistent shadows reduce maturity estimation reliability despite high detection confidence.
